verdicts.evidence_quote: Vendor may process customer data in any region where Vendor maintains infrastructure, including the United States, Ireland, and Singapore. Vendor will use reasonable efforts to keep EU data within EU regions but does not guarantee residency.
verdicts.note: Allows US and Singapore processing of EU data and provides only reasonable-efforts language rather than a guaranteed EU residency commitment.

verdicts.evidence_quote: The customer may audit Vendor's information security controls once per calendar year, on not less than 60 days' written notice.
verdicts.note: Audit notice period is 60 days, which falls in the 31–90-day unclear range; policy requires ≤30 days for a pass.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Termination for convenience is permitted by either party on 90 days' written notice.
verdicts.note: Termination for convenience is permitted on exactly 90 days' notice, meeting the policy maximum.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Vendor's aggregate liability under this Agreement is capped at fees paid by the customer in the 12 months preceding the event giving rise to the claim. This cap does not apply to liability arising from a data breach caused by Vendor's gross negligence.
verdicts.note: Cap meets the 12-month minimum, and data-breach liability caused by gross negligence is explicitly uncapped, which exceeds policy requirements.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Vendor maintains a list of subprocessors at vendor.example/subprocessors. Vendor will update the list within 30 days of any change.
verdicts.note: Contract only requires updating a public list within 30 days after a change; it provides no prior written notice to the customer before a new subprocessor is added.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Vendor will notify the customer within 96 hours of confirming a security incident affecting the customer's data.
verdicts.note: Notification window is 96 hours, exceeding the 72-hour policy maximum.

verdicts.evidence_quote: This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of California, United States.
verdicts.note: California has a recognised data-protection regime (CCPA/CPRA) so is not a violation, but it is not among the named preferred jurisdictions; consider seeking alignment to Delaware or New York.

verdicts.evidence_quote: All customer data will be processed exclusively within the European Economic Area (EEA). Globex will not transfer customer data outside the EEA without the customer's prior written consent.
verdicts.note: Exclusive EEA processing with an explicit prohibition on transfers outside the EEA without prior written consent.

verdicts.evidence_quote: The customer may request an audit of Globex's security controls on 30 days' written notice, not more than twice per calendar year.
verdicts.note: Audit right available on exactly 30 days' notice, meeting the policy requirement.

verdicts.evidence_quote: The customer may terminate for convenience on 60 days' written notice.
verdicts.note: Customer may terminate for convenience on 60 days' notice, well within the 90-day policy maximum.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Globex's aggregate liability is capped at the fees paid in the 12 months preceding the claim. The cap does not apply to liability for data breach, gross negligence, or wilful misconduct.
verdicts.note: Cap meets the 12-month minimum, and data-breach liability is explicitly excluded from the cap (unlimited vendor exposure for breaches).

verdicts.evidence_quote: Globex will provide the customer with at least 30 days' written notice before engaging any new subprocessor.
verdicts.note: At least 30 days' written notice before any new subprocessor is engaged, exactly meeting the policy minimum.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Globex will notify the customer within 24 hours of detecting a confirmed security incident affecting the customer's data.
verdicts.note: 24-hour notification window is well within the 72-hour policy maximum.

verdicts.evidence_quote: This Agreement is governed by the laws of England and Wales.
verdicts.note: England and Wales is explicitly listed as an approved jurisdiction in the policy.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Customer data is stored in Sirius's data centres in Malaysia and Singapore. Sirius may relocate data between its facilities at its sole discretion for operational reasons.
verdicts.note: Data stored exclusively in Malaysia and Singapore with no EU residency provision for EU customer data.

verdicts.evidence_quote: The customer may, on not less than 180 days' notice and not more than once every two years, conduct an audit of Sirius's security controls.
verdicts.note: Audit requires 180 days' notice, far exceeding the 90-day policy threshold that triggers a violation.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Termination for convenience is not permitted during the initial term.
verdicts.note: Termination for convenience is explicitly prohibited during the 5-year initial term.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Sirius's aggregate liability under this Agreement is capped at three (3) months of fees paid. This cap applies to all claims, including those arising from data breach or loss.
verdicts.note: Liability cap is only 3 months of fees, far below the 12-month policy minimum.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Sirius may engage any subprocessor it deems appropriate without prior notice to or consent from the customer.
verdicts.note: Contract explicitly permits adding subprocessors without any prior notice, directly violating the 30-day written notice requirement.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Sirius will notify the customer of confirmed security incidents in due course, taking into account the nature and circumstances of the incident.
verdicts.note: 'In due course' provides no specific window; the policy explicitly treats this phrasing as a violation.

verdicts.evidence_quote: This Agreement is governed by the laws of Malaysia.
verdicts.note: Malaysia is explicitly cited in the policy as a weak-enforcement jurisdiction.
